feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Extended Warehouse Management
feed.subtitle: SAP Extended Warehouse Management Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Warehouse Spare Parts SAP Mail Notification
entry.content:
Hello

I would like to ask if there is a way to link warehouse spare parts SAP with mail?

I would like SAP to send mail notification when we take 1 piece of the spare parts & to send how many stock still available.

 

Thank You.

entry.title: Flexible Batch Scan when entering count for PI Document in RF
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

During the PI count by WO in RF, the user is directed to count material X, for a specific batch presented by the system.  The order of batches being presented causes the user to walk around the pallet searching for each batch to be scanned many times.  This causes inefficiencies with counting a material that is batch managed when multiple batches exist in the bin / on a physical pallet.  

Is there a standard configuration that allows user the the ability to scan the batches in the physical order found on the pallet? So, the user should be able to scan the batch of his choice or at least have the ability to skip the batch that is being proposed by the system and come back to counting it later. Thanks.

entry.title: Date of Manufacturing in SAP EWM Inbound delivery of a purchase order
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

If minimum remaining Shelf life data is maintained in the master data for of Batch managed product then system expects the DOM in the GR. If the shelf life data is not maintained then DOM is not expected in the delivery.

The problem is if the DOM is not maintained in /scwm/prdi then we get the Queue error.
I do not want to make the field as mandatory in delivery profile because for the products where Shelf life data is not maintained business do not want to enter the DOM.  

Is there a standard way to not allow GR posting if shelf life data is maintained in the master data until DOM is entered in EWM inbound delivery ? or Is there a way to copy the GR date as DOM date at time of GR if user do not enter the SLED/DOM in /scwm/prdi ?

Alternate solution can be to create custom class  "Class for Field check" and assign it in incompleteness profile.

entry.title: Storage Bin is not getting blocked when PI document is created using Exception Code
entry.content:
Hi, 

I have configured to trigger Unplanned PI document when a specific Exception Code is used in RF Pick. I have checked the "Physical Inventory Block" Check box and yet the PI Block is not getting set when the document gets created.

entry.title: How can I use PMR with more than one storage locations?
entry.content:
Hello Community,

in order to seprate the stock in a AFS availabe for sale location and for production i want to create my psa's in a seperate storage location. And i want to use the new extended production integration with EWM PMR.

So i tried to set a a control cycle with staging indicator 5 and I wanted to set 2 storage locations

AFS -> 11P1 

so the material lies i a high rack and should be brought to production in another sloc. There the stock is safe and could not be sold thorugh a sales order / outboudn delivery due to ATP/picking location settings.

 

But: system does not allow this setup, even if both slocs are ewm managed under the same warehouse number.

 

any idea how to achive the scenario anyways?

 

 

br, doepat

entry.title: PO for inter-company repair and return process
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Can you please help with the types of PO to be used for inter-company repair and return process, apart from CCSTO/SSTO(NBA type: C and D). Material-A being shipped from one plant to another plant for repair, in the return Material number has been change to B (Unit price vary) due to configuration requirements and this is affecting financial on repair plant. So we thought of sending the material for repair through PR and PO process. since same equipment number need to be returned back to origin upon repair completion,  it is difficult to identify the difference between regular buying-PO and R&R-PO. So we need your expert advise the right process to follow.

entry.title: Do I need to map new SD Routes also in EWM in an embedded System?
entry.content:
Hello,

Do I need to map new SD Routes also in EWM in an embedded System? I know there is an customizing for external decentral ewm but are there any settings in case ov an embedded system?

thx

br

doepat

entry.title: EWM - Picking WT creation fails due lack of stock
entry.content:
Hi All,

In WH monitor I see the stock existing for a product and batches assigned to it. While creating Picking WT, this stock is not considered and WT creation fails . It gives the messages that Batch restricted stock is not considered for the storage types. 

Can anyone please confirm, why batch stock is not considered although batch is given during WT creation. I tried with and without batch but it gives same error 'No Source bin found'

entry.title: SAP EWM Physical Inventory count sheet Print Automatically after PI Document Active
entry.content:
The PI Count Sheet is required to Print automatically once the Physical Inventory Document is Active. The PPF Action is maintained with Action Profile /SCWM/PI_COUNT, but Printing is only possible manually via Transaction /SCWM/PRPI_DOC or Print Button in /SCWM/PI_PROCESS.

Thanks in Advance for the Solution.

entry.title: Handling Unit Label Printing Through PPF in SAP EWM
entry.content:
Hi all,

We have a business requirement to trigger the PPF HU for HU label print after the Goods receipt is posted. In standard, the HU gets printed immediately after the packing is done (HU is created and saved). Is there any way to trigger the PPF HU after GR is posted ?

Thank you for your help,

Regards,

entry.title: How to configure picking strategy M in storage type record?
entry.content:
Hi, SAP community,

What are the steps involved in configuring picking strategy "M" in storage type records? for small / large quantities.

Thanks!

entry.title: EWM Posting as Own stock for consignment articles, but correctly in ERP
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I am facing an issue while posting the STO returned to DC in EWM.
Scenario:
We are processing a return delivery STO from Store to EWM managed DC.
Whereas for the consignment articles the returns are posting as own stock in EWM, but the ERP postings are correctly posted as 161k and 671k movement.

Note: Using Retail consignment process

Something strange I could not figure it out, how come system is posting as OWN in EWM and passing the GR confirmation to ERP with K indicator.

The material master setup correctly as per Retail consignment Logic https://help.sap.com/erp2005_ehp_07/helpdata/en/aa/e2c353b677b44ce10000000a174cb4/content.htm?loaded_from_frameset=true 
Anything I am missing in EWM, also I noticed the stock usage C is not picking for this Return STO's consignment articles, though warehouse attributes are mapped with consignment indicator.

Any quick suggestion will be helpful, as this is creating dual ownership for same article.

entry.title: Parametrização de dois tipos de processo de recebimento de mercadoria para a mesma planta
entry.content:
Atualmente tenho um warehouse onde centralizo grande parte do meu estoque lá. Esse nosso warehouse fica proximo a nossa fabrica, porem entre o warehouse e a fabrica temos armazens para estoque de alto giro para atender a fabrica. Tambem na fabrica temos um pequeno estoque de baixo giro, porem não é o nosso foco armazenar lá.
Bom, com base nas informações acima, gostaria de saber como posso parametrizar o SAP com dois processo que acontece atualmente na parte de recebimento de material (compras), dando entrada no estoque do warehouse ou amarzem:
1 - Para processos de alto giro/prioridade: Os materiais são entregues diretamente nos armazens, onde acabam nem passando pelo warehouse, devido a morosidade no processo de entrega e saida para fabrica.
2 - Para processos de medio giro/prioridade: Os materiais são entregues para o warehouse, e apos o recebimento e organização, quando solicitado, são enviados para os armazens e consequentemente para fabrica.

Minha dúvida é, como faço para parametrizar o SAP com esses dois tipos de recebimentos? 
Isso é, sem a necessidade de programação "ABAP" ou qualquer tipo de parametrização para um usuário comum não possa fazer. SAP ECC 6.0

entry.title: Negative & Positive Entries Stuck in TR-Zone (922) in LS24 And Can be Confirmed via LT06
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

In the process of moving some units of a defective material from a WM to IM sloc via MIGO_TR, we now have a situation where in LS24, we have positive & negative entries of the material stuck in TR_Zone (LT22) and the system won't let us do the confirmation via LT06. We're struggling to understanding why the negative and positive entries are not cancelling out and leaving just the difference. We have checked and there are no existing PCNs. I can see that there are other stock of the same item in LS24 but with different storage types (just additional information in case it matters). 

Can someone please provide some guidance on how to resolve this issue so we can perform the confirmation, get rid of the separate negative and positive entries, so that the transaction can be completed?

Thanks in advance.

Bunie

entry.title: Configuring SAP for Two Types of Material Receipt Processes in a Warehouse and Storage Environment
entry.content:
Hi everyone!

Current situation:

We have a warehouse where most of our inventory is centralized.
This warehouse is located near our factory, but between the warehouse and the factory we have high-turnover stock storages to supply the factory.
We also have a small low-turnover stock at the factory, but it is not our focus to store there.

Requirement:

Based on the information above, we would like to know how to configure SAP with two processes that currently happen in the material receipt (purchasing) area, entering the stock in the warehouse or storage:
For high-turnover/priority processes: Materials are delivered directly to the storages, bypassing the warehouse due to the slow delivery and exit process to the factory.
For medium-turnover/priority processes: Materials are delivered to the warehouse, and after receipt and organization, when requested, they are sent to the storages and consequently to the factory.

Question:

How can we configure SAP with these two types of receipts without the need for ABAP programming or any type of configuration that a common user cannot do? (SAP ECC 6.0)

The goal is to find a solution that can be implemented by a regular user without the need for specialized technical knowledge.

entry.title: MAINTAIN A STORAGE LOCATION
entry.content:
HELLO EXPERT

I would like to know what is the purpose of <<MAINTAIN>> in SAP in general, and also more particularly for a Storage location ?

 

 

Thanks

entry.title: How to delete condition tables in testsystem
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

What is the theory behind creating a condition table (e.g., pack specification) in the development system? The IMG activity where we enter the table layout not only creates our tables locally. Our input is also saved in a customizing transport, right?

When we import this transport into the next system, the system generates the same database tables locally again. And our table layout ALSO creates a table locally in $TMP in the test system, correct?

But what if I want to delete the condition table from the test system again? What steps do I need to take? This is not possible with a transport because the system has also created it locally.

Thank you.

entry.title: HU information from S4 Outbound delivery not passed to EWM Outbound Delivery Order
How to prevent creating warehouse tasks for some storage types
entry.content:
Hello Experts, I need to prevent creating warehouse tasks for some storage types by unauthorized users. 

Due to my tests, this authorization depends on the TOCR object.

However, these users still have permission to create warehouse tasks despite they do not have LGTY attributes of those storage types.

I do not have this problem in the QA system but exists in the Production system.

Waiting for your kind responses.

entry.title: Shipping to Vendor for repair and return
entry.content:
Hi,

How can we send the material to vendor for repair and return.

Currently i am using ZLO(Offline process) which does not show equipment movement in SAP and the status shows the equipment still at my plant whereas i have moved it to vendor for repair. Upon receipt of the physical equipment back to my plant, we are performing stock return process in SAP and bringing the equipment to inventory stock.

I am looking for brief transaction movement or process to move the equipment to vendor in SAP, and later need to receive it in my plant's active stock upon physical receipt from the vendor.

Couple of questions (Raised by expert):

  1. Is your Equipment also mapped to Serial Number # ? 
  2. Have you explored standard Refurbishment process means -- Order (bring serial to inventory using 262)-->Sub Con PR-- Subcon PO--Transfer Posting 541O (Ship to Vendor) tracking available in MBLB--then receive 101 back to inventory 

point 2 needs split valuation if your org do not have split valuation --you can still explore Subcon process with serial numbers --

Response for above question raised by the expert:

Yes my equipment is serialized component. Can you please elaborate the steps required as i am bit confused. Regular Purchase Requisition created against the local job order(work order) but without material number--> Regular PO  created through the same PR --> 541 movement in MIGO to ship to vendor --> Shipping the physical component to vendor through ZLO-DT/Shipment --> Receiving the physical unit back from the vendor --> Perform GR(using MB1B-101) for the PO(initially created without material number). Then the serial number will reflect in my stock location (OR) do i need to perform separate movement to bring the unit in to my stock? Please correct me if i am wrong.

Thanks in advance.
